findings.evidence: `rg -n "isCharacteristicVector_iff|isCharacteristicVector_canonicalCharacteristic"` shows bare declarations at lines 69-81, while the rubric requires normal-form characteristic lemmas to carry `@[simp]` and grind-driving lemmas to carry `@[grind]`.
findings.fix: Add appropriate automation attributes, e.g. `@[simp]` on `isCharacteristicVector_iff` and `isCharacteristicVector_canonicalCharacteristic`, and a forward `grind` annotation for the vertex-wise parity implication if this file expects `grind` to use characteristic hypotheses.
findings.issue: The defining characteristic restatement and canonical characteristic fact are public characteristic API but unannotated, so `simp`/`grind` will not normalize `P.IsCharacteristicVector k` or close the canonical case.

summary: The characteristic-covector surface has the needed arbitrary-vector characterization, but its basic normal-form API is not annotated for automation. Add the expected `simp`/`grind` attributes to the defining characteristic lemmas so downstream lattice-homology code can use the predicate without manual unfolding.
